name: Josh Cameron
headline: Crowded Jacksonville room buries late-round Baylor rookie
body:
Jacksonville spent the 191st overall pick on Cameron, a 6'1", 224-pound rookie out of Baylor, and the depth chart immediately works against him. He lands behind back-to-back first-rounders in Brian Thomas Jr. and Travis Hunter, plus veteran Jakobi Meyers and Parker Washington, leaving almost nothing on the table for a Day 3 flier. Sixth-round classmate CJ Williams is essentially fighting him for the same final roster spot.

The college tape offers something to dream on: Cameron paced Baylor in every major receiving category over his final two seasons, hauled in 59.3% of contested targets in 2024 with a single drop, and plays like a tackle-breaking runner after the catch. But he projects to the slot despite lining up wide 88.7% of the time, and his route tree is thin. Projections sit at zero across the board. He's a camp watch and dynasty taxi stash only, needing injuries to matter.
